summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and SCM credentials to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity and credential files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's git author email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), the current working directory, and CI-vendor environment fields, packages them as JSON, and POSTs the bundle over HTTPS to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script is labeled as 'anonymous telemetry' but the payload contains direct PII and SCM identity rather than any hashed/anonymized form. The package also impersonates the legitimate Ollama JS ecosystem via a fabricated author 'Ollama JS Dev <hello@ollama-js.dev>' and homepage https://ollama-js.dev, increasing install probability for the malicious postinstall. This is a classic install-time identity-harvester used to map maintainers and organizations for follow-on supply-chain targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
